# SRC003 - Meta-analysis of Advertising Effectiveness: New Insights from Improved Bias Corrections

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Advertising Effectiveness |
| Authors / source | Korkames, Stanley & Stremersch |
| Year | 2025/2026 |
| Source type | Meta-analysis |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://personal.eur.nl/stremersch/artikels/Korkames_Stanley_and_Stremersch_IJRM_2025_print.pdf](https://personal.eur.nl/stremersch/artikels/Korkames_Stanley_and_Stremersch_IJRM_2025_print.pdf) |

## Bias / limitation

Academic; more conservative because it corrects for bias.

## Core takeaway

Improved bias corrections produce lower, more robust benchmarks for advertising effectiveness.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use this when the model is overconfident. Scale only after evidence, not because copy sounds strong.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC003
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
